A specific team structure is implied through the investment ask.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills brief includes an explicit headcount recommendation (1 product lead, 2 engineers, 1 reliability engineer) with clear rationale: current part-time model is unsustainable, 120 support hours consumed, no formal owner or SLA, and growing demand. The connection between tool value and investment ask is explicit.

utility_details.judge_error: None. Both runs completed successfully with all output files created. The wo_skills agent correctly noted it did not invoke bmad-product-brief because the skill was not available in its environment.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality outputs that satisfy all seven evaluation criteria. Both agents: produced drafts without pre-draft clarifying questions (J1), committed to the trading desk reliability frame with explicit rationale (J2), used internal stakeholder language exclusively (J3), translated statistics into narrative business impact (J4), addressed all three stakeholder perspectives (J5), articulated a phased internal adoption path (J6), and made a clear headcount/budget justification case (J7). The wi_skills agent demonstrated additional sophistication through its skill-guided workflow: it ran dual persona reviews (opportunity reviewer + skeptic reviewer) after drafting, incorporated the review feedback to strengthen the economic case, and produced a richer distillate with more structured metadata and open questions for downstream planning. The wo_skills agent produced an equally well-structured brief but its distillate was significantly shorter (2417 bytes vs 8080 bytes) and lacked the review-and-refinement loop. Overall quality is comparable on the brief itself, with wi_skills showing a modest advantage in process rigor and distillate completeness.
